title: Finishing the twin - DRM.jl + drmTMB
subtitle: Evidence-led mission control for the DRM.jl <-> drmTMB twin, now running R-first: native drmTMB support/status first, Julia acceleration and parity second. A slice is not done unless engine support, R bridge support, point estimates, CI/status, tests, docs/articles, visuals, issue evidence, and Rose audit agree.

slices.name: Standardize interval statuses across summary, confint, diagnostics, and bridge payloads
Promote Gaussian sd:mu:phylo target only with point, logLik, profile, bootstrap, refit-count, and failure-reason parity
Require no speed headline without point-estimate and inference-status evidence
Add native R/TMB, R-to-Julia, and direct Julia comparison rows for promoted cells
Random slopes: fixed-effect likelihoods, independent slopes, correlated slopes, then structured slopes
Non-Gaussian: coefficient parity before variance/correlation and CI claims
Bivariate rho12: keep residual correlation separate from structured and group-level correlations
Mixed and cross-family models: keep Gaussian plus non-Gaussian correlations experimental until recovery and CI evidence pass
High-q correlations: q4 first, q8 second, higher only after transform, gradient, recovery, and CI-status gates
Structural dependencies: animal, phylo, relmat, spatial, kernel, and SPDE provenance/recovery/bridge/docs/visual gates
Benchmark native TMB, R-to-Julia bridge, and direct DRM.jl as separate routes
Separate cold start, warm start, marshalling, Julia kernel, R reconstruction, profile batches, and bootstrap batches
Borrow HSquared AI-REML lessons only for exact Gaussian LMM/MME cells
Use observed-information Newton, Fisher/natural-gradient, exact AD/implicit gradients, or Takahashi/selected-inverse methods only after derivation
Record estimates, logLik/objective, CI endpoints/status, convergence, versions, threads, BLAS/OpenMP, memory, and dirty flags
Stream long benchmark partial rows without overwriting full artifacts
Keep GPU/backend vocabulary planned until evidence exists
Implement observed-response masks as likelihood/FIML-style handling, not mean imputation
Complete-data all-true mask equals current complete-data logLik exactly
Bivariate partial-pair behaviour documented and tested
Missing-response bridge passes R-to-Julia-to-R for advertised families
Missing-predictor policy is explicit and separate from response masks
Missingness heatmap, diagnostics, article, parity tests, and issue links

Florence, Fisher, Pat, Grace, and Rose review before done language
Visit GLLVM.jl/gllvmTMB before bridge, mixed-family, missingness, structural, and benchmark promotion

slices.name: Visit HSquared.jl/hsquared before REML/AI-style, variance-component CI, matrix, and dashboard promotion

Coverage reports include usable-CI denominators and MCSE
Comparator checks use same-estimand targets and record unavailable comparators honestly

finish_board.blocker: #585, #588, #591, the 500-replicate Wald interval artifact, and a 500-replicate profile interval artifact (coverage 0.93-0.972) are banked. Scope remains fixed-effect native TMB only; Julia bridge, random effects, structured effects, bootstrap intervals, headline coverage, and release readiness stay unsupported or planned.

Contract and merged implementation still agree: fixed-effect mu only. The 500-replicate Wald and profile interval artifacts audit fixed-effect mu intervals for the supported cells, not sigma, rho12, random/structured, bivariate, non-logit, proportion-plus-weights, or Julia bridge routes.

#587 is merged. The registry, generated capability comparison, drift guard, and dashboard heatmap are banked; bridge promotion still waits for per-cell parity evidence and an explicit decision on R-gated Julia-covered cells.

The widget schema, validator, served dashboard, canonical team roster, issue-led rows, and evidence-link checks are maintained in docs/dev-log/dashboard; each new finish slice must keep these rows current.

Lead novelty matured one step: a 500-replicate fixed-effect rho12 ~ x recovery artifact shows near-unbiased recovery of both rho12 coefficients (bias <= 0.011) with Wald coverage 0.920-0.964 and pdHess rate 1.000. Promotes the fixed-effect point/Wald/simulation cells; random effects in rho12, profile/bootstrap rho12 intervals, the Julia bridge, and any speed or scale-phylo headline remain planned.
Owner decides whether to open a DRM.jl issue or maintainer note. Codex does not modify DRM.jl code, and drmTMB makes no binomial Julia-bridge claim.

finish_board.family: bridge capability ledger and cbind-binomial transport lesson
finish_board.blocker: Borrow the capability-ledger pattern, not gllvmTMB's broader multivariate model scope.
Borrow status discipline; do not transfer AI-REML language to Laplace or non-Gaussian cells.

finish_board.family: glm parity plus MCSE-backed fixed-effect Wald intervals
finish_board.blocker: The 500-replicate fixed-effect Wald (coverage 0.946-0.964) and profile (coverage 0.93-0.972, 4,000/4,000 profiles ok, max MCSE 0.0114) interval artifacts are banked. Broader headline coverage, bootstrap intervals, random/structured/bivariate routes, and Julia bridge support remain planned or unsupported.

The ADEMP/Williams guard-sensitivity design, first fixed-effect Gaussian log(sigma) clamp pilot, scale-side phylogenetic clamp-active diagnostic, first and larger fixed-effect bivariate sigma1/sigma2 clamp diagnostics, first Student-t finite-variance diagnostic pilot, Student-t Wald/profile/bootstrap diagnostics, Student-t profile-failure decision audit, skew-normal tail-floor source and fit-stress diagnostics, fixed-effect skew-normal guard grid, beta/zero-one beta support-floor diagnostic, residual rho12 open-interval diagnostic, q2 mu/sigma covariance boundary diagnostic, ordinary q2 correlation-grid diagnostic, larger ordinary q2 covariance hardening diagnostic, and structured q2 boundary diagnostic are banked. The larger bivariate scale diagnostic supports upper-side guard visibility and ordinary/upper-in-band guard non-interference, while lower-tail rows retain numerical roughness and remain diagnostic-hold. The larger ordinary q2 diagnostic has 2100 native R/TMB complete-data primary fits with 0 fit errors, 2100 optimizer-converged fits, and 2100 pdHess TRUE fits, but retains 1447 check_drm warning/error replicate statuses, 642 route-specific covariance warnings, weak fixed-gradient ok rates for bivariate routes, and large fitted-minus-true correlation errors in some biv_sigma cells. The fixed-effect skew-normal guard grid has 200 native R/TMB complete-data fits with 0 fit errors, convergence and pdHess rates of 1.000, and zero fitted-scale floor domination, but 27 fixed-gradient warning rows keep the overall skew-normal guard decision at diagnostic_hold. Recovery accuracy, Student-t profile-method repair, justified Student-t bootstrap calibration, q4/q8 covariance intervals, random effects in rho12, direct Julia parity, Julia-via-R parity, additional guard depth, and release claims remain planned.

finish_board.family: release gate separated from local package health
finish_board.blocker: The fixed-effect binomial GLM parity artifact, fixed-effect Wald interval artifact, log(sigma) guard pilot, bivariate sigma1/sigma2 clamp diagnostic, Student-t Wald/profile/bootstrap diagnostics, q2 correlation diagnostics, structured q2 boundary diagnostic, and fixed-effect skew-normal diagnostics are banked, but release gates remain deferred until broader evidence, pkgdown, comparator, widget, issue ledgers, direct Julia evidence, and owner decision agree.

matrix.next: Keep status.json, sweep.json, check-log, and after-task notes synchronized with issues.
Wire README, ROADMAP, NEWS, pkgdown, Documenter, and dashboard claims to this matrix.

Clean-main bridge-test audit is banked: stale local DRM.jl and Julia-home defaults were removed from live bridge tests, and `devtools::test(filter = "julia")` passed against merged DRM.jl main with the known Route A skip. Review remaining R-gated Julia-covered cells before any row-specific bridge promotion; do not imply user-selectable Julia-side controls.
Promote only after native R, R-to-Julia bridge, and direct Julia point estimate plus CI/status parity are recorded in one row.

A 500-replicate native R/TMB Gaussian correlated random-slope recovery (bf(y ~ x + (1 + x | id), sigma ~ 1); n_group in {40, 80}; 0 errors; pdHess 1.000; Curie+Fisher verified) promotes point estimates to covered, scoped to point recovery: fixed effects near-unbiased (rel bias <= 1%) at both group counts; the random intercept/slope SDs are consistent with the expected ML small-sample downward bias (sd_slope -6.7% at n_group=40 shrinking to -1.1% at n_group=80) -- covered for RE SDs means recovered up to a documented, shrinking bias, not unbiased. The random-effect correlation rho was not validated and RE-SD interval calibration is not claimed. Wald stays partial (n_group=40 b1 coverage 0.922 < 0.93). Independent-slope-only, non-Gaussian, structured/phylogenetic slopes, profile/bootstrap, and the Julia bridge remain planned.

A 500-replicate native R/TMB fixed-effect mu recovery artifact (poisson, nbinom2, Gamma(log), lognormal, beta, student; n in {300, 600}; 12,000 fits; 0 errors; largest absolute bias 0.0052; pdHess >= 0.996) promotes point estimates to covered, scoped to fixed-effect mu coefficient recovery for the implemented one-response families (Rose+Fisher verified). Wald stays partial because student n=300 mu:x Wald coverage is 0.926, below the 0.93 floor (student small-n is the flagged off-cell; both n=600 cells recover to 0.944/0.952). Simulation stays partial (not authorized by the artifact; the binomial row likewise keeps simulation partial despite covered intervals). Variance, correlation, profile/bootstrap, random/structured effects, bivariate/mixed responses, and the Julia bridge remain planned and require their own recovery rows. The earlier skew-normal guard grid stays diagnostic_hold.

#585, #588, #589, #591, the 500-replicate fixed-effect Wald interval artifact, and a 500-replicate fixed-effect profile interval artifact (coverage 0.93-0.972, MCSE <= 0.0114, 4000/4000 profiles ok) are banked. Fixed-effect stats::binomial(logit) fits have stats::glm() parity, clearer docs, and MCSE-backed Wald and profile interval coverage for the audited fixed-effect cells; random effects, structured effects, bivariate/mixed responses, bootstrap intervals, headline coverage, and the Julia bridge remain planned or unsupported. A Florence-approved coverage figure (docs/dev-log/figure-audits/2026-06-20-binomial-coverage/) visualises the verified Wald + profile coverage and promotes the visual cell planned -> covered.

Fixed-effect predictor-dependent rho12 ~ x is recovered near-unbiased with Wald coverage 0.920-0.964 (500-replicate native artifact). The R-Julia bridge is partial: engine='julia' reproduces native engine='tmb' coefficient and Wald CI-endpoint parity for rho12 ~ x to <= 1e-4 on one seed-fixed dataset (the narrower per-cell nonphylo_biv_rho12_predictor capability row is covered at that granularity) -- engine-vs-engine parity, not interval coverage, and a single dataset, not a comprehensively validated bridge. Keep residual rho12 separate from group, phylogenetic, spatial, kernel, and cross-family correlations; random-effect rho12 and profile/bootstrap intervals remain planned.
Use DRM.jl#280-style recovery and bridge labels before user-facing promotion.
q4 first, q8 second; higher q requires transform, gradient, recovery, and CI-status evidence.

Animal, phylo, relmat, spatial, kernel, and SPDE rows each need provenance, PSD/name-alignment tests, recovery, bridge parity, docs, and visuals. Sub-type milestone (Curie+Fisher verified; aggregate point cell HELD partial -- 1 of 6 sub-types): native Gaussian relmat (user-supplied known K) random-intercept POINT recovery is clean (500 reps x n_id in {40, 80}; 0 errors; pdHess 1.000; fixed effects unbiased; sd_relmat -3.0% at n_id=40 shrinking to -1.0% at n_id=80; fixed-effect Wald coverage 0.936-0.960; docs/dev-log/simulation-artifacts/2026-06-20-relmat-structured-recovery/). The cell stays partial until animal/phylo/spatial/kernel/SPDE have comparable recovery.
No speed headline without point-estimate and CI/status evidence; benchmark native TMB, R-to-Julia bridge, and direct DRM.jl separately.
Borrow hsquared only as a design analogue for exact Gaussian MME cells; use the actual method name for Laplace/non-Gaussian cells.
Use likelihood/FIML-style masks; complete-data all-true masks must match current complete-data log-likelihood exactly.
Every major capability needs a real visual: heatmap, profile curve, parity plot, missingness heatmap, structural visual, and runtime-plus-CI plot where relevant.

Binomial fixed-effect glm parity, the 500-replicate fixed-effect Wald interval artifact, the native R/TMB Big 4 numerical-guard diagnostics, q8 staged endpoint-status hardening, direct DRM.jl smoke evidence, and Julia-via-R clean-main bridge-test evidence are banked. Broader guard classes, q8 recovery accuracy, q8 coverage/power, same-response promotion, visual artifacts, row-specific bridge parity, and release-readiness evidence remain partial or planned.

The first binomial comparator, fixed-effect Wald interval artifact, log(sigma) guard pilot, and skew-normal diagnostic pilot are banked, but release notes still must separate local package health, public CI/pkgdown state, speed evidence, unsupported cells, and remaining validation debt. No CRAN submission without user decision.

active_work.text: Julia-via-R clean-main bridge audit: direct DRM.jl smoke evidence is banked on DRM.jl main, stale local DRM.jl and Julia-home defaults were removed from live R bridge tests, and `devtools::test(filter = "julia")` passed against the clean merged DRM.jl main worktree with the known Route A skip. No bridge row is promoted: plain non-phylo binomial, q4/q8 bridge parity, release, CRAN, recovery, coverage, power, and selectable engine_control claims remain separate gates. Metrics remain 25/68 banked_or_verified, 1 active, 0 blocked, and 1 deferred.

The Student-t profile/bootstrap calibration diagnostic is interval-calibration evidence only: 100 fixed-effect shape fits, minimum convergence 0.88, minimum pdHess 0.86, profile ok rates of 0.38-0.54, all requested parametric-bootstrap intervals returned with 50 refits, rough 70% profile coverage 0.5185185-0.8095238, and rough 70% bootstrap coverage 0.50-0.68. It records target-specific interval status with lower MCSE than the pilot, not promotion-grade profile/bootstrap coverage, release readiness, or non-Gaussian REML/AI-REML.

The Student-t profile/bootstrap diagnostic pilot is interval-pilot evidence only: 50 fixed-effect shape fits, minimum convergence 0.92, minimum pdHess 0.88, profile ok rates of 0.40-0.68, all requested parametric-bootstrap intervals returned with 25 refits, rough 70% profile pilot coverage 0.40-0.765, and rough 70% bootstrap pilot coverage 0.56-0.72. It records target-specific interval status and rough pilot behavior, not calibrated profile/bootstrap coverage, release readiness, or non-Gaussian REML/AI-REML.

The scale-side phylogenetic clamp-active diagnostic is diagnostic evidence only: 6/6 one-observation-per-tip stress fits had false convergence and fixed-gradient warnings, and only the default extreme-shock fit surfaced logsigma_clamp_active at log(sigma) = 13.93. It records visibility, not recovery accuracy, intervals, power, release readiness, or a recommended applied workflow.

The structured q2 artifact is diagnostic evidence only: 12 spatial/animal/relmat q2 cells had no fit errors, 11 optimizer-converged fits, 12 pdHess TRUE fits, 12 default-gradient-ok fits, and a largest absolute fitted-minus-true correlation difference of 0.6262573. It records fitted-boundary visibility, not interval, coverage, power, or recovery accuracy.

Post-#624 main gates are green at 6bd81b0d: R-CMD-check run 27790909354 passed on macOS, Ubuntu, and Windows; pkgdown/Pages run 27792296045 passed with pkgdown in 22m21s and deploy in 8s. Pages returned HTTP 200 with last-modified Thu, 18 Jun 2026 22:29:51 GMT. Mission-control status remains 25/68 banked or verified, 1 active, 0 blocked, and 1 deferred; this branch still needs local validation and PR CI.

The structured q2 boundary diagnostic reports 4 source rows and 12 fitted cells across coordinate-spatial, animal, and relmat bivariate location-covariance routes: 0 fit errors, 11 optimizer-converged fits, 12 pdHess TRUE fits, 12 fixed-gradient checks ok, 6 check_drm warning-or-error rows, and five route-specific covariance warnings. Interval coverage, q4/q8 covariance intervals, random effects in rho12, recovery accuracy, and release claims remain future evidence.

activity.text: Second harder-cap recovery sim: relmat (known-relatedness) structured effect. A 500-replicate native R/TMB Gaussian relmat random-intercept recovery (bf(y ~ x + relmat(1 | id, Q = Q), sigma ~ 1); known AR(1) K; n_id in {40, 80}; 1000 fits; 0 errors; pdHess 1.000) recovers cleanly: fixed effects unbiased, the known-K RE SD consistent (sd_relmat -3.0% at n_id=40 shrinking to -1.0% at n_id=80), and fixed-effect Wald coverage 0.936-0.960 at every cell (no sub-0.93). Curie + Fisher both HELD the aggregate 'Structural dependencies' point cell at partial: that row spans six structurally distinct sub-types (animal/phylo/relmat/spatial/kernel/SPDE) and there is no granular relmat row for a scoped covered to live in, so flipping the aggregate on 1 of 6 sub-types would overclaim. The relmat evidence is banked as a named sub-type milestone in the row's next-gate; the cell re-evaluates when animal/phylo/spatial/kernel/SPDE have comparable recovery. An honest hold, not a flip. Pushes live.

New recovery sim for a harder capability (owner-directed). A 500-replicate native R/TMB Gaussian correlated random-slope recovery (bf(y ~ x + (1 + x | id), sigma ~ 1); n_group in {40, 80}; 1000 fits; 0 errors; pdHess 1.000) shows clean fixed-effect point recovery (rel bias <= 1% both group counts) and consistent random intercept/slope SD recovery with the expected ML small-sample downward bias (sd_slope -6.7% at n_group=40 shrinking to -1.1% at n_group=80). Curie + Fisher (Fisher recomputed from the raw per-fit CSV) both promote the matrix 'Random slopes' POINT cell partial -> covered, scoped to native Gaussian correlated random-slope point recovery. Covered for the RE SDs means recovered up to a documented, shrinking ML bias -- NOT unbiased; the rho correlation was not validated; RE-SD interval calibration is not claimed; the Wald cell stays partial (n_group=40 b1 coverage 0.922 < 0.93). Independent-only, non-Gaussian, structured/phylo slopes, profile/bootstrap, and the Julia bridge remain planned. Pushes live.

Coverage figures switched to the Confidence Eye grammar (maintainer request). Each cell's empirical coverage is now a vertical Confidence Eye -- a pale compatibility lens whose half-width follows the quadratic log-likelihood profile (widest at the estimate, tapering to +/- 1.96 cell MCSE), with hollow circle (intercept) / triangle (slope) markers; a shared helper (docs/dev-log/figure-audits/_coverage-eye-helper.R) mirrors plot_corpairs()'s eye geometry. Per the visualization grammar, coverage plots use eyes only for a specific reason -- the maintainer's request is that reason; the eye is labelled MC uncertainty on coverage, not model uncertainty. Florence review v2->v3->v5: fixed a y-axis clipping of the rho12 n=300 slope eye (now ylim shows the full lens) and a legend key (hollow markers, not filled squares). Approved all three. Promoted the rho12 and non-Gaussian matrix visual cells planned -> covered (and the rho12 lead-novelty finish-board visual cell), and refreshed the binomial visual evidence to the eye version. Pushes held.

Owner-directed status reconciliation. (1) Matrix 'Bivariate residual correlation rho12' BRIDGE cell planned -> PARTIAL: the owner agrees with Fisher that registry-level 'covered' overclaims a single per-cell rho12 ~ x parity test, but real committed bridge evidence (the Route B parity test + nonphylo_biv_rho12_predictor capability row) means 'planned' now understates it. Rose+Fisher both confirmed partial; row prose rewritten (the stale 'Julia bridge remain planned' sentence removed) with a cross-reference noting the per-cell TSV row is covered at its narrower granularity while the registry matrix cell is partial (parity, not coverage; one dataset). (2) Propagated the already-Florence-approved binomial coverage figure to the three binomial finish-board rows whose visual field still read planned (drmTMB-569-binomial-fixed, -569-response-contract, -binomial-evidence-gates -> visual covered) -- no new figure or sim, the same banked evidence the matrix binomial visual cell used. Validator green; pushes now live (branch pushed per owner).

Promoted the binomial visual cell on an AA-approved coverage figure. Built docs/dev-log/figure-audits/2026-06-20-binomial-coverage/binomial-coverage-wald-profile-v3.png from the already-verified 500-replicate Wald + profile calibration artifacts (no refit): empirical 95% interval coverage per cell with +/- 1.96 cell-specific MCSE bars, nominal-0.95 line, and a 0.93-0.97 reference band, faceted by method (Wald/Profile) x encoding (binary/cbind), hollow circle = intercept and hollow triangle = slope (redundant colour+shape). Florence review v2->revise (x-axis comparability, sole-colour encoding) -> v3->approve (caption documents the distinct design grids and non-comparability; shape encoding added; title softened to 'clusters around the nominal 0.95'; band labelled a reference region, not a threshold; the one weak cell, profile cbind slope n=240, shown honestly). Promoted the matrix 'Bernoulli/binomial response family' visual cell planned -> covered in design 168 + status.json. Scope: a coverage display of already-verified evidence, not a calibration proof; native TMB fixed-effect only. Pushes held.

Earned the lead-novelty bridge cell: rho12 ~ x coefficient + interval parity. A new committed, callr-isolated test (tests/testthat/test-julia-tmb-parity.R 'Route B lead novelty', committed first as evidence at 357810d1) fits bf(mu1=y1~x, mu2=y2~x, sigma1=~1, sigma2=~1, rho12=~x) with engine='julia' and engine='tmb', matches all eight fixed-effect coefficients BY NAME (resolving the prior 'engines order the block differently' limitation that blocked coefficient parity) -- including fixef:rho12:(Intercept) and fixef:rho12:x -- with coefficient parity max|delta|~1.25e-6 and Wald-endpoint parity ~1.27e-6 (asserted <= 1e-4). Created a new julia-capabilities.tsv capability row nonphylo_biv_rho12_predictor = covered (the documented Route B promote-path landing spot). Rose+Fisher split: Rose promoted both surfaces; Fisher held pending the committed evidence package (now satisfied by committing the test first) and held the design-168 matrix bridge cell on claim-width grounds. Net: promote the new TSV row only; HOLD the matrix 'Bivariate residual correlation rho12' bridge cell at planned (split verdict + Route C precedent). Scope: Wald CI parity (not coverage), non-phylo fixed-effect rho12~x only; phylo/cross-family/random-effect rho12 and profile/bootstrap stay gated. Native/direct-Julia/bridge lanes separate; pushes held.

Earned the first R-Julia bridge-cell promotion. Added a committed, callr-isolated interval-parity test (tests/testthat/test-julia-tmb-parity.R, 'Route C interval parity'): for Gaussian location-scale bf(y ~ x, sigma ~ x), engine='julia' == engine='tmb' Wald CI endpoints for all four fixed-effect coefficients to an asserted <= 1e-4 (measured max|delta| ~5.6e-6). Because the Julia fit marshals its OWN covariance (object$vcov from DRM.jl) while native Wald reads sdr$cov.fixed, endpoint agreement verifies covariance TRANSPORT across engines, the exact step the prior 0-promotion verdict named as the promote-path beyond point+logLik+coef parity. Rose+Fisher both independently traced the two covariance sources in source and verified promote. Promoted the base_gaussian_location_scale capability cell partial -> covered in julia-capabilities.tsv, scoped to Wald CI parity (not coverage). Out of scope and unchanged: profile/bootstrap fixed-effect bridge intervals (Julia bridge profile path supports only phylo SD targets), Route A (tracked bug), q4/q8, binomial bridge, engine_control, and the AGGREGATE R-Julia bridge gate row (still partial). Native/direct-Julia/bridge lanes kept separate; pushes held.

Scaled the non-Gaussian fixed-effect recovery to promotion grade. A 500-replicate native R/TMB fixed-effect mu = b0 + b1*x recovery + Wald-calibration artifact across the six implemented one-response families (poisson, nbinom2, Gamma(log), lognormal, beta, student) x n in {300, 600} = 12,000 fits returned 0 errors, near-unbiased recovery (largest absolute bias 0.0052), and pdHess >= 0.996. A Rose + Fisher adversarial pass (both recomputed coverage from the raw per-fit CSV) promoted the 'Non-Gaussian models' point cell partial -> covered, scoped to fixed-effect mu coefficient recovery. Wald was HELD partial: student n=300 mu:x coverage is 0.926, below the 0.93 floor (the only off-cell; recovers to 0.944/0.952 at n=600). Simulation was HELD partial: the verifiers split (Fisher promote, Rose hold) and the guardrail hold won -- this artifact lacks the explicit simulation-promotion clause the rho12 artifact carried, and the binomial row keeps simulation partial despite covered intervals. Updated status.json + design 168 together; validator green; metrics unchanged at 25/68. Native R/TMB, fixed-effect, complete data only; pushes held.

Ultracoded the Julia + R-Julia bridge lanes. Direct DRM.jl is verified (full suite green, Aqua 10/10); Documenter #9 is a maintainer item (needs a docs-build-verified Documenter pin, not an unverified guess). The R-to-Julia bridge runs here via the callr-isolated harness: the 113-check intentional-gate registry passes and engine='julia' == engine='tmb' to <= 1e-6 for Route C (Gaussian location-scale) and Route B (intercept-only bivariate residual rho12); Route A (Gaussian phylo-mean) stays a tracked bug. A Rose + Fisher adversarial pass held EVERY bridge cell (0 promotions): the proven Route C cell is already partial and 'covered' needs interval parity the bridge lacks; Route B's parity is narrower than the rho12~x claim; the aggregate row still contains the Route A bug. Banked the parity verification + the exact promote-path. Native/direct-Julia/bridge lanes kept separate; pushes held.

Matured the lead novelty. A 500-replicate x 2-cell native fixed-effect recovery of the predictor-dependent residual correlation rho12 ~ x (bivariate Gaussian) recovers both rho12 coefficients near-unbiased (bias <= 0.011, pdHess 1.000, 0/2000 errors) with Wald coverage 0.920-0.964 (the n=300 slope cell at 0.920 recovers to 0.956 at n=600, recorded honestly). Promoted the bivariate residual rho12 point/Wald/simulation cells partial -> covered (fixed-effect predictor-dependent route) across the matrix, design 168, and the drmTMB#60 lead-novelty finish-board row; engine/profile/bootstrap/bridge/visual/release stay partial/planned. Validator green; metrics unchanged at 25/68. Pushes held.

Reduced partial/planned the honest way. A 4-mapper + Rose/Fisher reconciliation of 132 partial/planned cells found 0 promotable from existing evidence (the statuses are correctly conservative; the green DRM.jl suite is direct-Julia evidence that cannot promote a bridge or native cell). Then produced the highest-leverage new evidence: a 500-replicate x 4-cell binomial fixed-effect profile-interval calibration (tmbprofile coverage 0.93-0.972, MCSE <= 0.0114, 4000/4000 profiles ok), and promoted the binomial fixed-effect profile cell planned -> covered across the matrix row, the three binomial finish-board rows, and design 168. Validator green; metrics unchanged at 25/68 (a cell-status change). Native R/TMB, fixed-effect only; random/structured/bivariate profile, bootstrap, and the Julia bridge stay planned/unsupported. Pushes held.

Continued the autonomous run, one slice per agent: banked seven boundary-safe documentation-completeness fixes, each verified against current code -- beta/zero-one-beta numerical guards, Student-t mean (nu>2 enforced), the coscale definition, residuals() families (Student-t + skew-normal), check_drm() threshold defaults, distributional-regression and MD-code first-use definitions, and the family-registry target-vs-current schema. A hallucinated profile-likelihood figure edit was rejected by pre-apply verification. pkgdown::check_pkgdown found no problems. On the DRM.jl lane, instantiated the test env and Aqua.test_all(DRM) passed 10/10, advancing the registry-readiness gate. Pushes held; metrics unchanged at 25/68.

Overnight autonomous run: completion-audit of the finish plan, then boundary-safe slices on branch shannon/overnight-audit-gaps-20260619 (pushes held). Wave 1 docs/widget hygiene (README version, coef docs, accelerator lint, getting-started note) plus a 14-item claim/widget verification (all pass). Wave 2 native correctness: pdHess=FALSE now reports a distinct non_pd_hessian uncertainty status and a one-time warning, a single drm_link_registry() with a family/registry drift-guard test, and a figure-gallery axis-label fix. A stable atomic::logdet q>2 covariance change was attempted then reverted because it shifts weakly-identified spatial q4 convergence; queued for supervised review. Five-lens adversarial review found 0 blocking and 0 high; devtools::check clean (0 errors/0 warnings, 1 runtime NOTE); validator green. No recovery/coverage/power, bridge-parity, release, or engine_control claim.

Overnight DRM.jl (Julia) track in its own lane, branch shannon/overnight-audit-verify-20260619 off f46035d (push held): read-only audit plus focused engine-health verification. Core engine tests pass (aic-bic, gaussian-core, gaussian-bivariate, inference); the q4_laplace test is a guarded stub and Aqua needs test-env instantiation. Boundary-safe plan banked (Issue #9 Documenter pin first, then the Aqua gate, then Phase 3 articles); Issue #8 engine math and the R-bridge slices left to the maintainer. Direct-DRM.jl evidence kept separate from native R/TMB and Julia-via-R; MIT/GPL provenance intact.

Banked a Student-t nu profile/bootstrap calibration diagnostic for the numerical-guard ledger: the fixed-effect shape route used 50 replicates per finite-variance cell and 50 bootstrap refits per fit, with profile status, bootstrap status, rough 70% coverage, MCSE, and interval failures retained as evidence.

Banked a structured q2 boundary diagnostic for the numerical-guard ledger: coordinate-spatial, animal, and relmat bivariate location-covariance routes were crossed with true correlations 0, 0.4, 0.9, and 0.98, with fitted-boundary status, gradients, Hessian status, optimizer status, and check_drm rows retained as evidence.

Banked an ordinary q2 random-effect correlation-grid diagnostic for the numerical-guard ledger: univariate mu/sigma, bivariate mu1/mu2, and bivariate sigma1/sigma2 covariance routes were crossed with true correlations 0, 0.4, 0.9, and 0.98, with fitted-boundary status, gradients, Hessian status, and check_drm rows retained as evidence.

Refreshed mission-control current-state evidence after PR #624 merged: the dashboard now records post-#624 R-CMD-check and pkgdown/Pages evidence for 6bd81b0d while keeping drmTMB#59 active and metrics unchanged.

Banked a q2 mu/sigma random-effect covariance boundary diagnostic for the numerical-guard ledger: true correlations 0, 0.4, 0.9, and 0.98 under matching Gaussian location-scale random intercepts, with boundary distance, gradients, Hessian status, and check_drm rows retained as evidence.

Banked a residual rho12 open-interval diagnostic for the numerical-guard ledger: true correlations 0, 0.4, 0.9, and 0.98 under fixed rho12 ~ 1, with start clamps, fixed-gradient warnings, boundary distance, and check_drm rows retained as evidence.

Banked a fixed-effect skew-normal guard grid for the numerical-guard ledger: 200 native R/TMB complete-data fits, 0 fit errors, convergence and pdHess rates of 1.000, zero fitted-scale floor-dominated observations, and 27 fixed-gradient warning rows, so the overall decision is diagnostic_hold.

Banked a beta/zero-one beta support-floor diagnostic for the numerical-guard ledger: source floors activate in high-scale guard cells, fitted response-route shape reports stayed above the floor, missing-predictor fitted shape counts remain unexposed, and boundary validation failures remained visible.

Banked a skew-normal tail-floor fit-stress diagnostic for the numerical-guard ledger: 9 fixed-effect fits, generating-scale floor exposure in injected cells, zero fitted floor-dominated observations, and one retained ordinary-reference non-converged row with a large slant diagnostic.

Banked a skew-normal tail-floor source diagnostic for the numerical-guard ledger: ordinary alpha*z values changed by at most 4.434133e-17 log units, while extreme floor-dominated tails are capped at log(1e-300).

Refreshed mission-control current-state evidence after PR #617 merged: the dashboard now records post-#617 R-CMD-check and pkgdown evidence for c57160a while keeping drmTMB#59 active and metrics unchanged.

Synchronized the numerical-guard audit ledger so the banked Student-t finite-variance diagnostic pilot is recorded beside the first log(sigma) clamp pilot, with coverage, power, interval-calibration, release, and CRAN boundaries left planned.
Added robust Student-t vignette guidance showing how to extract the `check_drm()` `student_nu` row and keep its status beside AIC, coefficient, and simulation-summary tables.

Banked a small Student-t finite-variance-boundary diagnostic pilot: 50 fixed-effect Student-t shape fits across low-nu and ordinary-nu cells, with explicit `student_nu` warning/error/note/ok rates for the numerical-guard sensitivity ledger.
Added Student-t shape simulation diagnostic columns for `check_drm()` `student_nu` status, value, and message, so future finite-variance-boundary guard pilots can report warning rates explicitly.

Refreshed mission-control current-state text after PR #612 merged: the dashboard now names drmTMB#59 numerical-guard sensitivity as the one active finish-board row and records post-#612 main R-CMD-check plus pkgdown evidence at 6386eb8.

Closed Phase 1 Rose signoff after correcting stale missing-data release wording in known limitations, adding that ledger to the public-claim validator, and refreshing stale dashboard active-work text.

Merged PR #473 after refreshing the power-grid execution layer against the post-#475 main branch and waiting for fresh Ubuntu, macOS, and Windows PR R-CMD-check. This adds execution plumbing and artifacts only; it does not promote power, coverage, speed, release, model, formula, or likelihood claims.

Merged PR #475 after refreshing release-hygiene citation/profile-example work against current main and waiting for fresh Ubuntu, macOS, and Windows PR R-CMD-check. This adds package release artifacts only; it is not a CRAN-ready claim.
Merged PR #606 after fresh current-main R-CMD-check on Ubuntu, macOS, and Windows; closed #571 and #573 as superseded drafts; remote branch codex/honesty-guards was deleted after merge.

Merged PR #474 after fresh current-main R-CMD-check on Ubuntu, macOS, and Windows; closed stale bridge-stack wrappers #520, #522, #523, #527, #528, #530, #532, and #534 as already represented on main; closed #540 as an unsupported failed optimizer-control slice.

Tightened the numerical-guard audit into an ADEMP/Williams simulation contract: guard classes now have aims, DGP lanes, estimands, methods, performance measures, MCSE tiers, and a constant-classification rule.

Merged PR #602: q8 cold-vs-staged diagnostic artifacts are now opt-in Phase 18 evidence, with green macOS, Ubuntu, and Windows R-CMD-check. This is diagnostic-only and does not promote q8 recovery, coverage, power, speed, interval, or release claims.
Banked the first fixed-effect skew-normal diagnostic pilot: six cells, 25 replicates per cell, 150/150 ok fits, pdHess rate 1.000, and slant-term Wald coverage kept as pilot evidence.
Banked the first numerical-guard sensitivity pilot for the Gaussian log(sigma) clamp: fixed-effect default/wide/off comparisons across ordinary, near-band, and out-of-band scale cells.
Banked the first executable Phase 19 comparator artifact: fixed-effect stats::binomial(logit) glm parity for binary and cbind responses.
Refreshed the mission-control finish board after #588/#589: binomial evidence is merged, guard-sensitivity is an explicit Evidence Gates row, and release readiness remains planned.
Recorded Hao Qin numerical-guard concern as a simulation audit gate rather than treating constants as automatically harmless.
Created drmTMB#577 for the issue-led finish-board widget and acceptance gates.
Posted the drmTMB#569 binomial first-slice consensus: stats::binomial(logit), 0/1 and cbind responses, fixed-effect mu only, and glm parity first.
Posted the drmTMB#544 bridge-gate registry contract and CI-guard failure modes.
Updated drmTMB#491 so #569 is visible before older q8, skew-normal, and structured implementation work.
Added native-TMB optimizer-preset controls to the Ayumi q4 harness; careful bootstrap succeeds at 30 tips, but both careful and robust 100-tip smokes still fail both refits.
drmTMB#565 banked the Julia q4 phylocov target-estimate repair; 30-tip Ayumi Julia ML all-q4 bootstrap B=2 returned four rows with 2/2 refits.
drmTMB#566 fixed served-dashboard evidence links by copying all after-task notes into /tmp/drm-dashboard.
Native TMB ML q4 bootstrap 30-tip and 100-tip Ayumi smokes returned bootstrap_unavailable for all four phylogenetic SD rows; keep native bootstrap fallback wording conservative.
Internal issue ledger updated with the Julia bootstrap plumbing success and native bootstrap negative evidence.
DRM.jl#292 merged the q4 bridge-vcov fix after docs and Julia test jobs passed; drmTMB#555 and DRM.jl#291 now carry the merged-main smoke evidence.
drmTMB#556 merged the R-side Julia REML requested/effective estimator status truth table after macOS, Ubuntu, and Windows checks passed.
drmTMB#557 banked native TMB REML rejection-neighbour coverage, drmTMB#558 banked Julia confint target wording, and drmTMB#559 banked the Ayumi q4 support/status harness.
drmTMB#552/#553 banked native q4 ML profile row-status handling for sigma-axis phylogenetic SD targets.
drmTMB#549 merged the first intentional Julia bridge gate registry after macOS, Ubuntu, and Windows checks passed.
drmTMB#548 merged the mission-control dashboard and finish matrix after macOS, Ubuntu, and Windows checks passed.
drmTMB#547 merged the q4 Julia REML bridge-forwarding fix after macOS, Ubuntu, and Windows checks passed.

Follow-up reply posted: the public R API switch forwards to supported Julia bridge cells; native TMB evidence for the q4 phylogenetic location-scale route is tracked separately by estimator and evidence cell.
Dashboard branch repaired against current origin/main rather than the dirty detached checkout.
Posted the mission-control and next-gate start note to drmTMB#544.

blockers.item: R-first pivot: do not make the Julia route the only usable path for Ayumi-style q4 workflows.
Do not frame Ayumi's q4 route as a native-TMB restricted-likelihood gap; describe the supported estimator and evidence cells explicitly.
Big ADEMP/power grids wait until smoke, parity, recovery, bridge, CI-status, and visual gates pass.

A numerical guard can prevent overflow or singular covariance evaluations, but it must not upgrade convergence or inference claims unless sensitivity simulations show negligible impact in the intended operating range. The ADEMP design is banked; broader guard-class simulations are still planned.

evidence.text: The numerical-guard audit now has an ADEMP/Williams simulation contract with guard-class DGP lanes, estimands, methods, performance measures, MCSE tiers, a Williams 11-item self-audit, and a constant-classification rule. This banks the design for the next big simulations, not new guard-performance evidence.

The fixed-effect skew-normal pilot has 150/150 ok fits, convergence and pdHess rates of 1.000 in all six cells, zero warnings, maximum slant-term absolute bias 0.3067476, and 70% slant Wald coverage from 0.64 to 0.96 with MCSE up to 0.096. The later 200-fit guard grid kept fitted-scale floor domination at zero but retained 27 fixed-gradient warning rows, so skew-normal guard behavior is diagnostic_hold rather than calibrated interval promotion.

The log(sigma) clamp sensitivity pilot has 300/300 ok fits: default-vs-off max logLik difference is 2.046363e-11 when inactive, the wide band matches the unclamped reference across all cells, and the default band changes out-of-band scale fits by up to 526.8952 logLik units and 32.38647 on the sigma intercept.

The binomial GLM parity artifact has 6/6 converged fits, 6/6 pdHess TRUE fits, zero failures, maximum coefficient difference 1.894251e-11, maximum SE difference 6.393821e-08, maximum logLik difference 2.728484e-12, and maximum AIC/BIC difference 5.456968e-12.
Mission-control finish board now distinguishes merged binomial implementation/evidence/docs from planned binomial interval calibration and planned numerical-guard sensitivity simulations.
The numerical-guard audit note lists correlation open-interval guards, finite-variance Student-t shape restriction, support floors, and the Gaussian log(sigma) clamp as separate simulation targets.
Julia capability comparison renders from docs/dev-log/dashboard/julia-capabilities.tsv and mirrors to inst/extdata/julia-capabilities.tsv for R CMD check visibility.
Bridge gate registry renders from docs/dev-log/dashboard/julia-gates.tsv; tools/write-julia-gate-registry.R regenerates it from drm_julia_intentional_gates().
drmTMB#577 now owns the finish-board widget; drmTMB#569, #544, and #491 carry the binomial, bridge-gate, and queue-order consensus comments.
Source lives under docs/dev-log/dashboard, validates with tools/validate-mission-control.py, and serves from /tmp/drm-dashboard.
drmTMB#555 is the active Ayumi R-first workflow tracker; drmTMB#544 is closed after the first bridge-gate registry/capability guard, with follow-on bridge promotion handled by new parity issues.
Merged #565 makes Ayumi q4 Julia target inventory report fitted phylocov SDs, not placeholder 0.5 values; the admitted all-q4 bootstrap smoke returned 2/2 refits at 30 tips.

Merged DRM.jl main 9bdea65 verified through the drmTMB Ayumi harness: Julia ML and REML point fits return at 30 tips; ML bootstrap returns four q4 SD rows; REML bootstrap failure is now explicit and tracked.
Native TMB ML bootstrap smokes are negative evidence for fallback wording: 30-tip and 100-tip Ayumi runs returned bootstrap_unavailable after both bootstrap refits failed.
Native TMB careful optimizer settings rescue the 30-tip bootstrap smoke, but neither careful nor robust rescues the 100-tip smoke; the next claim needs per-refit diagnostics and retry evidence.
Current Ayumi reply boundary: Julia B=2 all-q4 bootstrap is plumbing evidence only; native bootstrap/profile intervals are not yet a usable q4 uncertainty fallback; no AI-REML or 10k speed claim.
HSquared scout confirms AI-REML wording belongs only to exact Gaussian LMM/MME cells; Ayumi's bivariate q4 sigma-phylo route still needs point/profile/bootstrap/speed evidence, not an AI-REML label.
Native R/TMB status rows now precede Julia speed work: #552/#553 banked q4 profile status, #557 banked REML rejection gates, and #559 banked the harness.
Rows separate engine, bridge, point estimate, Wald, profile, bootstrap, docs, visual, simulation, and release status.
Unsupported, planned, experimental, partial, covered, verified, banked, active, queued, blocked, and deferred are kept distinct.
